title: Fix the Missing Edge Case in Palindrome Checker

statement.markdown: Write a function that checks if a given string is a palindrome. A palindrome is a string that reads the same backwards as forwards. For example, 'radar' and 'level' are palindromes. Your function should return `true` if the string is a palindrome and `false` otherwise. Note that the function should be case sensitive and should consider spaces and punctuation as part of the string.
statement.notes: The function should work for strings of any length, including empty strings.

constraints.description: The length of the input string

examples.explanation: The string 'radar' reads the same forwards and backwards
The string 'level' reads the same forwards and backwards
The string 'hello' does not read the same forwards and backwards

hints.text: Start by comparing the first and last characters of the string. If they are not equal, the string is not a palindrome.

editorial.title: Palindrome Checker
editorial.intuition: The problem can be solved by comparing the first and last characters of the string, and then moving towards the center.
editorial.betterSolution: The problem can be solved in O(n) time complexity by comparing characters from both ends of the string.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a two-pointer approach, where one pointer starts from the beginning of the string and the other from the end.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution can be proved by considering all possible cases.
commonMistakes: Not considering the edge case where the string is empty
Not being careful when comparing characters
